criteria_results.criterion: Root layout.tsx title becomes template: { template: '%s — Stride', default: 'Stride — Process Mapping & Continuous Improvement' }
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/layout.tsx line 22-26: metadata.title = { template: '%s — Stride', default: 'Stride — Process Mapping & Continuous Improvement' } confirmed by static read

criteria_results.criterion: [workspaceId]/layout.tsx exports generateMetadata with title template '%s — {workspaceName} — Stride'
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/layout.tsx lines 6-25: generateMetadata fetches workspace name and returns { title: { template: `%s — ${workspaceName} — Stride`, default: `${workspaceName} — Stride` } } confirmed by static read

criteria_results.criterion: Canvas [tabId]/page.tsx uses tab name as title (dynamic via generateMetadata)
criteria_results.evidence: Live browser test: navigating to /w/c8f6619d-bdd8-4134-b7aa-3a70bcf599ea/fcfdce67-1453-46c9-a8f2-c3c0ef615619 shows page title 'Workflow — My Workspace — Stride' (tab is named 'Workflow')
Live browser test on page load: document.title = 'Workspaces — Stride' at http://localhost:3000/workspaces

criteria_results.criterion: /login and /signup pages export metadata with title 'Log In' and 'Sign Up'
criteria_results.evidence: Static read of src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx: export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Log In' }. BUILD_RESULT_1 notes signup/page.tsx was refactored to server component exporting metadata with title 'Sign Up'

criteria_results.criterion: When a preset is applied and availability masks >= 1 section, a muted info note appears below the preset buttons
criteria_results.evidence: Export PDF dialog opened on canvas page. Accessibility tree shows paragraph ref=e205 with text '4 sections unavailable — add data to unlock them' positioned below preset buttons (Executive Summary / Full Audit / Gap Report)

criteria_results.criterion: Note shows count of masked sections (e.g. '3 sections unavailable — add data to unlock them')
criteria_results.evidence: Note reads '4 sections unavailable — add data to unlock them' — count is accurate given disabled checkboxes: Improvements, Perspective Comparison, Prioritization Matrix, Tool Landscape

criteria_results.criterion: Note disappears when no preset is active or all preset sections are available
criteria_results.evidence: Implementation verified in BUILD_RESULT_2: maskedCount computed only when activePreset !== 'custom' && maskedCount > 0. Not live-tested due to action budget, but logic is sound

criteria_results.criterion: An 'Analysis' button appears in the tools canvas toolbar (alongside Add Tool, Add Tool Section)
criteria_results.evidence: Accessibility tree on /tools page shows toolbar panel (ref=e246) containing buttons: 'Add Tool' (ref=e247), 'Add Tool Section' (ref=e248), 'Analysis' (ref=e249) — all three present in order

criteria_results.criterion: Button text switches between 'Analysis' and 'Canvas' based on showAnalysis state
criteria_results.evidence: Button shows 'Analysis' in default state (showAnalysis=false). BUILD_RESULT_3 notes: 'toolbar Panel is only rendered when showAnalysis=false, so button always shows Analysis in practice; conditional text {showAnalysis ? Canvas : Analysis} is correct for completeness'

criteria_results.criterion: Existing sidebar analysis toggle continues to work
criteria_results.evidence: Accessibility tree on /tools page shows sidebar button 'Tool Analysis' (ref=e257) with 'Analysis' label still present in the Tools Summary sidebar panel — unchanged from prior implementation

improvements_found.observation: The toolbar Analysis button (IMP-104) always shows 'Analysis' because the toolbar panel is hidden when analysis view is active. Users switching back to canvas must use the sidebar toggle — the toolbar button only provides one-way navigation. The 'Canvas' label on the button is dead code in current architecture.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H1: Visibility of system status — users in analysis view have no toolbar affordance to return to canvas
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Either (a) render the toolbar panel inside the analysis view with 'Canvas' button visible, or (b) accept current behavior as intentional since sidebar toggle serves the return path
